content.front: How many main methods of customs valuation are provided under the Agreement?
content.back: Six methods.

content.front: Why must customs be careful where royalty payments relate partly to imported goods and partly to other factors?
content.back: Because an objective and quantifiable apportionment is needed before any royalty adjustment can be made.

content.front: What is meant by proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use under Article 8.1(d)?
content.back: A payment or value from later resale, disposal, or use of the imported goods that accrues directly or indirectly to the seller.

content.front: What is transaction value?
content.back: The price actually paid or payable for imported goods when sold for export to the country of importation, adjusted as required by Article 8.

content.front: What is the rule on insurance costs under Article 8.2?
content.back: Insurance may be included where it is part of the cost of bringing the goods to the place of importation, subject to the law and evidence.

content.front: If goods are supplied on loan and remain the exporter’s property, is there a sale?
content.back: No. That does not constitute a sale.

content.front: Do conditions relating to the production or marketing of the imported goods always cause rejection of transaction value?
content.back: No. Some such conditions do not prevent use of transaction value, for example certain buyer-supplied engineering or marketing activities on the buyer’s own account.

content.front: Give a second example of a problematic condition in Article 1.1(b).
content.back: Where the price of the imported goods depends on the price or prices at which the buyer sells other goods to the seller.

content.front: What is a buying commission?
content.back: A fee paid by the importer to the importer’s agent for representing the importer abroad in the purchase of the goods being valued.

content.front: Why is customs value important?
content.back: Because customs duties and taxes, especially ad valorem duties, are calculated using the customs value as the assessment base.

content.front: Must the seller be in the exporting country for transaction value to apply?
content.back: No. The key issue is whether there was a sale for export to the country of importation, not where the seller is located.

content.front: Can buyer and seller be related if both are controlled by a third person?
content.back: Yes.

content.front: Why does the Agreement prefer transaction value?
content.back: Because it is based on the actual commercial transaction and therefore reflects the real value of the imported goods as closely as possible.

content.front: Are all later payments by the buyer to the seller treated as proceeds under Article 8.1(d)?
content.back: No. The payment must relate to the imported goods being valued and not merely be some other financial flow.

content.front: How is the value of an assist determined if it was produced by the importer or a related person?
content.back: By using the cost of producing it, limited to relevant costs and excluding profit elements.

pairs.left: What is Article 8 mainly about?
pairs.right: Adjustments to the price actually paid or payable when determining transaction value.

pairs.left: Name one category of related persons under Article 15.
pairs.right: Members of the same family.

pairs.left: When can royalties and licence fees be added to customs value?
pairs.right: When they relate to the goods being valued, are paid as a condition of sale, and are not already included in the price actually paid or payable.

pairs.left: What is Method 6 of customs valuation?
pairs.right: The fallback method.

pairs.left: How are warranty costs treated when already included in the selling price of imported goods?
pairs.right: They form part of the customs value because they are part of the price actually paid or payable.

pairs.left: Give a third example of a problematic condition in Article 1.1(b).
pairs.right: Where the price is based on a form of payment extraneous to the imported goods, such as supplying finished goods back to the seller.
